concerns.text: Tasks T035, T036, and T037 implement Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and confidence extraction. The spec (FR-001 to FR-013) and Success Criteria (SC-001 to SC-007) contain NO requirement, metric, or success criterion for measuring model confidence, calibration, or ECE. These tasks implement unapproved scope creep not traceable to any FR/SC.

Task T018 ('Add logic to handle reduced sample size') is vague. The spec (US-1, Edge Cases) requires flagging reduced sample size in the final report, but T018 does not explicitly map to generating the specific report section or state flag required to satisfy the acceptance criteria. A more specific task (e.g., 'Update report generator to flag reduced sample size') is needed to ensure coverage.

Task T024 ('Implement error classification logic') and Task T034 ('Implement error classifier for stratified sampling') create ambiguity. FR-010 requires classifying failures for a stratified sample. T024 (US-2) suggests raw tagging, while T034 (US-3) suggests analytical sampling. The tasks do not clearly distinguish the scope (raw data tagging vs. statistical sampling logic) to ensure full coverage of FR-010 without duplication or gaps.

Task T038 ('Generate final report') aggregates metrics including calibration (from T035-T037). Since T035-T037 are not required by the spec, T038's scope is partially unanchored. The task should be refined to explicitly list only the spec-mandated metrics (pass@1, McNemar, Mixed-Effects, Sensitivity) to ensure the report generation task strictly covers FR-006 through FR-013.
